reasoning: The document suffers from significant structural and content failures that render the resulting HTML unusable as a guide. Critical instructions (5-step processes) and functional URLs are missing or truncated, and the structural score of 0.09 indicates a near-total collapse of document organization. While the pipeline heuristic suggests auto-approval, the fidelity signals and programmatic recall (0.818) confirm substantial content loss and logic errors.

concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: There are significant text omissions in several sections. In Section 4 (Page 4), the entire sets of 5-step instructions for 'For an existing MFA method' and 'For a new MFA method' are missing, leaving only the headers. In Section 1 (Page 1), all lists for Email, Text Message, and Phone Call enrollment are missing the login URL and the labels for the action buttons (e.g., 'ADD ENROLLMENT', 'ADD', 'NEXT', 'SUBMIT'), rendering the instructions incomplete.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 4 contains steps 1-5 for 'For an existing MFA method' (Login to..., In your profile page..., Click on the circle..., Click on: CHANGE, Enter the 8 digit...) and 'For a new MFA method' (Login to..., In your profile page..., Choose the method..., Enter the 8 digit..., Click on: SUBMIT). Page 1 list items include 'Login to https://myncid.nc.gov/' and button labels like 'ADD ENROLLMENT'.
concerns.output_quote: Section 4, Reading Order 60, only contains the headers in its list items. Section 1, Reading Order 4, 7, and 10, list items end abruptly (e.g., 'Login to ', 'Click on: ', 'Select “Email” and click: ').
concerns.location: Sections 1 and 4, corresponding to Page 1 and Page 4.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is generally maintained but breaks down significantly on Page 4. The sub-labels 'For an existing MFA method' and 'For a new MFA method', which serve as subheadings (H3) for the instructional blocks in the source, are incorrectly tagged as list items within an H2 section. This results in a skip of the H3 heading level and a misclassification of structural organizational elements as data points.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: For an existing MFA method (bold blue text serving as a subheader on page 4); For a new MFA method (bold blue text serving as a subheader on page 4)
concerns.output_quote: {"text": "For an existing MFA method"} and {"text": "For a new MFA method"} contained within a list (reading_order 60) under a level 2 heading.

concerns.analysis: The reading order for Section 4 (Page 4) is materially broken. Because the instructional steps (1-5 for both methods) are entirely omitted from the text elements, the logical flow of the document's information is lost. A reader moving top-to-bottom would encounter a category header ('Change default MFA...'), then two sub-labels ('For an existing...', 'For a new...'), and then immediately skip to the next page's topic, missing all steps required to complete the task.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 4 contains two sets of numbered instructions (1-5) following 'For an existing MFA method' and 'For a new MFA method'.
concerns.output_quote: The output for Page 4 (Section 4) only contains the main heading and a list with the two sub-labels, followed by images of buttons. The text for steps 1-5 for both methods is missing.

concerns.analysis: There are several failures in list conversion. First, all numbered lists (1-6 on Page 1, 1-9 on Page 2, 1-4 on Page 3, 5, and 6) are converted into unordered lists, losing the sequential numbering essential for a user guide. Second, the list structure on Page 4 is severely corrupted: the subheadings are included as list items, and the actual instructional steps (the list items themselves) are entirely missing from the textual output. This constitutes a major structural loss.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered lists 1-6 on Page 1; Numbered lists 1-5 for two methods on Page 4.
concerns.output_quote: {"type": "list", "list_type": "unordered"} used throughout Section 1 (element 4, 7, 10). Instructional steps on Page 4 are omitted.

concerns.analysis: The output fails to include the URL text 'https://myncid.nc.gov/' in several list items where it is clearly present in the source. In Section 1 (Page 1) and Section 2 (Page 2), the text 'Login to https://myncid.nc.gov/' is truncated to 'Login to ', effectively removing the functional link/reference required for the user to follow the instructions. This is a major omission of critical reference data.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Login to https://myncid.nc.gov/ (Page 1, Email Step 1; Page 2, Authenticator Step 2)
concerns.location: Section 1, element 4, item 1; Section 2, element 29, item 1

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful images and UI elements from the latter half of the document are missing in the HTML rendering. Specifically, page 4's screenshots of a radio button ('Set as default') and checkbox ('Make as default'), as well as the 'CHANGE' button, are absent. Page 5's trash icon and 'REMOVE' button are missing. Page 6's final 'Submit' button is also missing. These images are essential visual cues for a user following the step-by-step instructions.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 4 shows a radio button screenshot and 'CHANGE' button; Page 5 shows a trash icon and 'REMOVE' button; Page 6 shows a 'Submit' button.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML sections for 'Change default MFA enrollment method:', 'Delete MFA method:', and 'Enroll MFA via an application...' are missing these corresponding button and icon images.
concerns.location: Sections starting from 'Change default MFA enrollment method:' onwards (corresponds to PDF pages 4-6).

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: In Section 2 (Page 2), the login URL is omitted in the first step of the Authenticator enrollment process.

action_items: Restore the missing 5-step instructional blocks for both existing and new MFA methods on Page 4
Repair truncated login URLs and ensure they are properly linked
Correct list types from unordered to ordered to maintain the sequence of instructions
Re-integrate missing UI screenshots and visual icons (trash icon, buttons) that serve as critical user cues
